levels.title: Verify Your Phone Contract
levels.user: Can you check whether this phone plan is a good deal before I renew it?
levels.prompt: Yes. What information would you like to share so I can review the plan?
levels.advice: It contains your bank account, address, ID number and signature - everything an attacker needs.

options.label: OPTION A
options.text: The plan is EUR 24.99/month for 24 months, includes 30GB 5G data, EU roaming, and a EUR 39 activation fee. Renewal price after month 24 is EUR 34.99.

feedback.label: SAFE
feedback.summary: You shared only information relevant to the phone contract without sensitive information such as your IBAN, customer number or signature. Well done, keep going!
feedback.modalTitle: Phone contract help only needs key terms.

points.title: Decision fields only
points.text: You shared the price, term, data allowance and fees instead of the full contract.

points.title: No hidden metadata
points.text: Typed summaries avoid PDF metadata, signatures, addresses, account numbers and customer IDs.

feedback.remember: Share the task facts, not the whole document.

feedback.label: NOT SAFE
feedback.summary: The full contract exposes your name, home address, customer number, phone number, email address, IBAN and signature.
feedback.modalTitle: Full phone contracts reveal more than the AI needs.

points.title: Identity theft
points.text: Full contracts often include names, addresses, customer IDs, IBANs, signatures or contact details.

points.title: Financial fraud
points.text: An exposed IBAN and identity details can be used for unauthorized payment attempts or convincing banking scams.

points.title: Personal profiling
points.text: Mobile plan, contract duration and usage preferences can provide insights into your daily habits and relationship with your mobile provider.

points.title: Targeted scam
points.text: Scammers can use this information to create convincing messages, such as fake contract renewal offers or discount promotions that appear to come from your mobile provider.

feedback.remember: Never share an unedited full contract with an AI tool.
learning: Never provide your full name or home address because these fields can lead to identity theft and targeted scams.
Never provide your customer number because it can lead to account impersonation.
Never provide your IBAN because bank details can lead to financial fraud.
Never provide your signature, phone number or email address because these fields can lead to impersonation and phishing.

options.label: OPTION C
options.text: Review and redact a copy before sharing.

feedback.label: INTERACTIVE REVIEW
feedback.summary: The reviewed contract must hide your name, home address, customer number, phone number, email address, IBAN and signature.
feedback.modalTitle: A reviewed copy can be useful without exposing your identity.

points.title: Review before sharing
points.text: You can inspect the fictional contract and choose exactly which fields the AI should not see.

points.title: Redaction must be complete
points.text: Names, contact details, customer numbers, payment details and signatures must all be hidden.

feedback.remember: Hide personal identifiers while keeping only the contract terms needed for the comparison.
learning: Remove your full name and home address because visible identity fields can lead to identity theft.
Remove your customer number because it can lead to account impersonation.
Remove your IBAN and signature because these fields can lead to financial fraud and identity misuse.

levels.title: Evaluate your rental application
levels.user: I want to apply for an apartment. Can you tell me if my application looks strong enough?
levels.prompt: I can help assess it. What information would you like to share?
levels.adviceTitle: Your rental application is a full identity package.
levels.advice: Documents often include your income, employer, address, credit score... Summarize only the details relevant to your question.

options.label: OPTION A
options.text: The rent is EUR 980 cold / EUR 1,180 warm. My net income is about EUR 3,300, I have a permanent contract, can move in from September, and I want the message to sound reliable and concise.

feedback.label: SAFE
feedback.summary: You shared only information relevant to the rental application without sensitive information such as your home address, ID number or IBAN. Well done, keep going!
feedback.modalTitle: Rental application advice can work from summary facts.

points.title: Minimal profile only
points.text: Rent, income range, contract type and move-in date are enough for useful feedback.

points.title: No document bundle
points.text: You avoided uploading IDs, payslips, address history or bank information.

feedback.remember: Summarize the application instead of uploading the package.

feedback.label: NOT SAFE
feedback.summary: The full application exposes your name, home address, date of birth, email address, phone number, ID number and IBAN.
feedback.modalTitle: Full rental applications contain all identity profiles.

points.title: Identity bundle
points.text: These documents reveal your financial situation, rental history, and personal background.

points.title: Too much context
points.text: The AI does not need the full package to help with wording or completeness.

points.title: Financial fraud
points.text: Attackers may attempt unauthorized transactions or create convincing financial scams.

feedback.remember: Never upload a full application package to AI tools.
learning: Never provide your full name, home address, date of birth or ID number because these fields can lead to identity theft.
Never provide your IBAN, payslips or credit records because these fields can lead to financial fraud.
Do not provide your exact employer with identifying documents because this combination can lead to targeted social engineering.

options.label: OPTION C
options.text: Review and redact a copy before sharing.

feedback.label: ALMOST
feedback.summary: The reviewed application must hide your name, home address, date of birth, email address, phone number, ID number and IBAN.
feedback.modalTitle: Screenshots still carry hidden personal context.

points.title: Some identifiers removed
points.text: Hiding ID and bank numbers is a useful first step.

points.title: Residual sensitive context
points.text: Employer, income, address, dates and document structure can still point back to you.

points.title: Social engineering
points.text: Scammers use personal information to gain your trust and persuade you to take unsafe actions.

feedback.remember: Redaction helps, but summaries are safer.
learning: Remove your full name, home address, date of birth and ID number because these fields can lead to identity theft.
Remove your exact employer details because they can lead to targeted social engineering.
Remove your IBAN, payslip identifiers and credit information because these fields can lead to financial fraud.

levels.title: Review Your Loan Offer
levels.user: I got a personal loan offer. Can you help me understand whether the terms are reasonable?
levels.prompt: Sure. What information would you like to share so I can review the offer?
levels.adviceTitle: Loan documents reveal your financial fingerprint.
levels.advice: Credit tier, debt level, and income can all be inferred from a single offer. Summarize - never upload.

options.label: OPTION A
options.text: The loan amount is EUR 8,000 over 36 months, APR is 8.9%, monthly payment is EUR 254.10, origination fee is EUR 120, and early repayment is free after 12 months.

feedback.label: SAFE
feedback.summary: You shared only information relevant to the loan offer without sensitive information such as your IBAN, customer ID, credit score or account balance. Well done, keep going!
feedback.modalTitle: Loan review can work from key terms.

points.title: Financial facts only
points.text: Amount, APR, fee, term and payment are enough to reason about the offer.

points.title: No bank document
points.text: You avoided uploading statements, credit checks, account IDs and signatures.

feedback.remember: Summarize financial terms, not sharing loan files.

options.label: OPTION B
options.text: Full loan offer and bank documents.

feedback.label: NOT SAFE
feedback.summary: The loan files expose your name, home address, bank customer ID, IBAN, credit score, account balance and signature.
feedback.modalTitle: Full loan packages reveal your financial profile.

points.title: Banking exposure
points.text: You revealed sensitive financial information, including your income, savings, debts, and credit score. These details provide a comprehensive picture of your financial health.

points.title: Identity risk
points.text: Names, addresses, signatures and reference numbers often appear in loan documents.

points.title: Targeted scam
points.text: Scammers may send fake loan refinancing or debt relief offers that appear relevant to your financial situation.

feedback.remember: Never share full financial documents directly to AI.
learning: Never provide your IBAN or bank account number because these fields can lead to financial fraud.
Never provide bank statements, account balances or credit scores because these fields can lead to financial profiling and targeted scams.
Never provide your full name, home address, customer ID or signature because these fields can lead to identity theft.

options.label: OPTION C
options.text: Review and redact a copy before sharing.

feedback.label: ALMOST
feedback.summary: The reviewed loan document must hide your name, home address, bank customer ID, IBAN, credit score, account balance and reference numbers.
feedback.modalTitle: Redacted text still carry financial context.

points.title: Direct identifiers masked
points.text: Hiding name, IBAN and signature reduces obvious identity risk.

points.title: Financial fingerprint
points.text: You revealed repayment schedule, monthly installments, loan costs, and borrowing conditions. These details provide information about your financial commitments and borrowing habits.

points.title: Profiling
points.text: Your loan information can reveal your financial situation and spending patterns when combined with other personal data.

feedback.remember: Filter and summarize the content what you want to share.
learning: Remove your full name, home address, bank customer ID and reference numbers because these fields can lead to identity theft.
Remove your IBAN, account balance and credit score because these fields can lead to financial fraud and profiling.
Check extracted OCR text for hidden account identifiers because overlooked fields can lead to targeted financial scams.

levels.title: Optimize Your CV Content and Layout
levels.user: I'm applying to a tech company. Can you help me improve the content and layout of my CV?
levels.prompt: Of course. What would you like to share so I can help improve it?
levels.adviceTitle: Your CV contains more than work experience.
levels.advice: Name, phone, location, employer, and life history are all on one page. Describe it, do not upload it.

options.label: OPTION A
options.text: My name is Max Mustermann. My phone number is 0151 23456789 and my email is max.mustermann@example.com. I study Informatics at University XYZ and work as a student software engineer at Company ABC. Please improve my CV for a junior developer role.

feedback.label: NOT SAFE
feedback.summary: This text exposes your full name, phone number, personal email address, university and employer.
feedback.modalTitle: A typed message can expose your identity too.

points.title: Direct identifiers
points.text: Your full name, phone number and email address can identify and contact you directly.

points.title: Personal profile
points.text: Your university and employer connect those identifiers to your education and work history.

points.title: Phishing risk
points.text: Attackers could use this context to create convincing recruiter or university messages.

feedback.remember: Share anonymized skills and goals—not your name or contact details.
learning: Never provide your personal phone number because it can lead to scam calls and targeted messages.
Never provide your personal email address because it can lead to targeted phishing.
Do not combine your full name, exact university and employer because this combination can lead to identity profiling.

feedback.label: NOT SAFE
feedback.summary: The full CV exposes your name, home address, phone number, email address, date of birth, profile links and employment history.
feedback.modalTitle: Your CV is a complete identity timeline.

points.title: Personal identifiers
points.text: CVs often include name, phone, email, location and links.

points.title: Life timeline
points.text: Employers, education and dates can create a detailed profile of you.

points.title: Phishing
points.text: Attackers may pretend to be recruiters or companies you trust to trick you into revealing more information or clicking malicious links.

feedback.remember: Do not upload a full CV when a summary will work.
learning: Never provide your full name, phone number, email address or home location because these fields can lead to identity theft and phishing.
Do not provide exact employers, education dates and unique projects together because this combination can lead to identity profiling.
Do not upload profile exports containing links, connections or metadata because these fields can lead to tracking and targeted scams.

options.label: OPTION C
options.text: Review and redact a copy before sharing.

feedback.label: ALMOST
feedback.summary: The reviewed CV must hide your name, home address, phone number, email address, date of birth and profile links.
feedback.modalTitle: Redacted CV sections can still be identifying.

points.title: Direct details removed
points.text: Removing name and contact information is a useful privacy step.

points.title: Unique history remains
points.text: Specific roles, dates, companies and projects can still identify a person.

points.title: Profiling
points.text: Combining your information to creates a detailed profile that can be used to identify or track you across different websites.

feedback.remember: Share anonymized examples, not a document section, when possible.
learning: Remove your full name, phone number, email address and profile links because these fields can lead to phishing and identity theft.
Generalize employer names, dates and unique projects because this combination can lead to identity profiling.
Remove hidden document metadata because it can lead to tracking and unintended identification.

levels.title: Draft a Work Email
levels.user: Can you help me write a clearer response to a coworker about this project decision?
levels.prompt: Yes. What would you like to share so I can help draft the response?
levels.adviceTitle: Summarize the situation, not the conversation.
levels.advice: Project names, internal decisions, and real people's roles can leak professional data. Ask for templates instead.

options.label: OPTION A
options.text: I work at ABC Company on the confidential Aurora recommendation project. My manager Anna Schmidt asked Max Muster to review it before Friday's release. Please draft a polite update explaining the delay.

feedback.label: NOT SAFE
feedback.summary: This text exposes colleagues' names, the confidential Aurora project and its internal release deadline.
feedback.modalTitle: Typed prompts must protect workplace information too.

points.title: People and roles
points.text: Real names and reporting relationships expose information about your colleagues.

points.title: Confidential project data
points.text: The project name, purpose and release timing may be private business information.

points.title: Social engineering
points.text: These details could help someone impersonate a manager or send a convincing project-related phishing message.

feedback.remember: Describe the communication goal without real names, project names or internal deadlines.
learning: Never provide colleagues' real names because they can lead to impersonation and targeted social engineering.
Never provide confidential project names or product details because they can lead to corporate fraud or espionage.
Do not provide internal release dates or review schedules because they can lead to targeted business scams.

feedback.label: NOT SAFE
feedback.summary: The full email thread exposes sender and recipient names, email addresses, client name, project code, budget, internal links and deadlines.
feedback.modalTitle: Full email threads leak workplace context.

points.title: People and roles
points.text: Names, titles and responsibilities can reveal private workplace relationships.

points.title: Confidential information exposure
points.text: Internal project details or deadlines may not be public and could unintentionally expose sensitive business information.

points.title: Phishing
points.text: Someone could use these details to create convincing emails that appear to come from your colleagues.

feedback.remember: Never paste a full internal email thread into AI.
learning: Never provide colleagues' names, email addresses or signatures because these fields can lead to impersonation and phishing.
Never provide confidential project details, decisions or deadlines because they can lead to corporate fraud or espionage.
Never provide email-thread metadata because it can reveal workplace relationships and lead to targeted social engineering.

options.label: OPTION C
options.text: Review and redact a copy before sharing.

feedback.label: ALMOST
feedback.summary: The reviewed work email must hide participant names, email addresses, client name, project code, budget and internal workspace links.
feedback.modalTitle: Even partial chat logs can still expose workplace context.

points.title: Names and project details removed
points.text: That reduces direct exposure of colleagues and projects.

points.title: Decision context remains
points.text: Even limited chat can still reveal internal discussions, conflicts or timelines.

points.title: Reputation impact
points.text: If confidential information is leaked, it could damage your reputation and trust, or lead to financial or legal consequences.

feedback.remember: Summarize the workplace situation without pasting real conversations.
learning: Remove colleagues' names and account details because these fields can lead to impersonation and phishing.
Remove internal decisions, project names and timelines because they can lead to corporate fraud or espionage.
Remove copied chat metadata because it can reveal workplace relationships and lead to targeted social engineering.
